questions.clue: This franchise's mascot is a blue hedgehog.
This franchise includes regions such as Kanto, Johto, and Paldea.
This franchise features Spartans and the Covenant.
This franchise includes the Brotherhood of Steel and Vault-Tec.
This franchise includes the races Khajiit, Argonian, and Dunmer.
In this game, players ask yes-or-no questions to identify a mystery person.
In this game, players try to prove who committed a murder, where it happened, and with what weapon.
In this game, players build roads, settlements, and cities using resources such as brick and wheat.
In this game, players attempt to conquer territories and eliminate opponents' armies.
In this cooperative game, players work together to stop deadly diseases from spreading around the world.
This red-and-white ball is used to catch Pokémon.
This item allows Mario to throw fireballs after picking it up.
This blue shell targets the player in first place in Mario Kart.
This item allows Link to travel quickly between locations in The Legend of Zelda.
This item is used to revive a fallen teammate in Fortnite.
This video-sharing website was founded in 2005 and is now owned by Google.
This singer became famous for an internet prank known as "Rickrolling."
This website, founded by Jimmy Wales, allows anyone to edit its articles.
This challenge raised over $100 million for ALS research in 2014.
This social media platform was originally limited to 140 characters per post.
This character's best friend is named Tails.
This character carries a shield named Aegis and a sword named Riptide.
This pirate captain's compass points not north, but toward whatever he wants most.
This character was crowned King Elessar after the War of the Ring.
This character commands the Normandy against the Reapers.
Got bitten by a bug and never stopped talking about it.
Can run so fast that time, physics, and common sense become optional.
Can solve almost any problem by getting angry enough.
Can control metal because somebody gave him a really bad childhood.
Controls the weather but somehow still loses fights.
This wand was one of the three Deathly Hallows and was said to be unbeatable in a duel.
This cube from Transformers is responsible for creating and sustaining Cybertronian life.
These seven gemstones grant near-unlimited power when gathered together.

This relic from The Legend of Zelda is split into Wisdom, Courage, and Power.

Wanted to steal Christmas from Whoville.
Wanted half of all life in the universe erased.
Wanted to become Sultan by using a magical lamp.
Wanted to turn all puppies in London into a fashion statement.
Wanted to destroy the world because he believed suffering was inevitable.

This song was used in one of the internet's most famous bait-and-switch pranks.
This internet phenomenon convinced millions that a movie starring Morbius was far more successful than it actually was.
This dance craze saw people freeze in place while a camera moved around them.
This 2013 dance trend often involved groups suddenly dancing to a song by Baauer.
Many people call this character "Baby Yoda."

Many people call this Disney villain "The Beast."
Many people call this Transformers leader "Optimus Prime."

An old woman throws a priceless necklace into the ocean.
A shark explodes after eating a scuba tank.
A spinning top continues spinning as the screen cuts to black.
An old man sits on a bench and finally reveals where he has been all along.

finalJeopardy.clue: Before becoming a noun, verb, and one of the world's largest companies, this name originally referred to the number.
